text: From hiking to hotpot, lonely consumers in China fuel a $9.4 billion companionship economy The increasingly popular service is part of a broader "companionship economy" emerging in China, which includes paid partners for running, sightseeing and even eating out at hotpot restaurants – a meal traditionally shared with friends. Providers, often students or young gig workers, advertise on social media with promises of "emotional value", conversation and practical help, turning what was once an experience or favour among friends into a bookable – and payable – service. "Most of my clients are women and their core need is emotional value, someone who makes them feel good and makes the trip easy," he said. REUTER If only someone built something to serve women... if only

text: Exploring the Design Space for a Post-Quantum Public Key Registry for Ethereum Validators How Ethereum is preparing for quantum computers Quantum computers could break today's BLS signatures. Ethereum is adding a simple "Public Key Registry" first - a safe warmup phase so validators can register tiny new quantum-proof keys before the real switch Validators register one 52-byte public key (almost same size as now) that lasts ~1,600 years. Bigger signatures are bundled using fast zero-knowledge proofs so the network stays fast and cheap Keys are generated offline in a few hours on a normal computer (tiny secret to back up). Registration is rate-limited with early rewards and later penalties - smooth, gradual, and open for easy community tools

text: Explains the selloff today? Justin Drake on Quantum Big quantum breakthrough + secrecy fail: Google found a huge 10x speedup to break Bitcoin/Ethereum signatures, but hid the details with a secret "zero-knowledge proof" after talking to the US government. text: Binance Research: U.S. Stocks Are Siphoning Capital From Crypto Binance Research, the institutional-grade research arm of Binance, said crypto's recent weakness may be driven less by crypto-native factors and more by capital being pulled into U.S. equities. It noted that the Cboe Dispersion Index has risen to 42, the third-highest level on record, showing S&P 500 flows are heavily concentrated in a few themes such as AI, semiconductors, defense, energy and commodities, leaving BTC increasingly sidelined. Binance Research said that in past periods of extreme U.S. equity concentration, BTC typically bottomed within 0 to 20 weeks, with a median of about two weeks; without a crypto-native crisis, such capital diversion is often temporary. — link

text: Polymarket Strategy Bitcoin-Sale Bet Sparks Dispute After "No" Ruling Polymarket's market on whether Strategy sold Bitcoin before the end of May resolved to "No" after drawing about $118 million in volume, triggering community debate over the ruling. Some users questioned whether Polymarket's later explanation changed how the original rules were applied. Polymarket said it was aware of the dispute and would issue any clarification by 1 p.m. ET on June 1; if no statement was released by then, the team would provide no further clarification, and the market would proceed to settlement at that time. — link

text: Onchain privacy best practice Blockchains are fully public by default, but privacy protocols decouple your identity from transactions by pooling funds in shared smart contracts where withdrawals cannot easily be linked to deposits True anonymity requires disciplined habits, including using different addresses, varying transaction amounts, letting time pass between actions, and masking your IP address to prevent analysts from mapping your digital footprint

text: Kelp DAO Hacker Has Laundered Nearly All $220M in Unfrozen Funds, Closing the Recovery Window According to The Defiant, on-chain tracking data shows that the hackers behind the Kelp DAO bridge exploit, identified as North Korean threat group TraderTraitor, have laundered approximately $220 million in unfrozen funds through privacy-focused channels including THORChain, Wasabi, Tornado Cash, and Umbra. Only around $1.7 million remains in the hackers' original wallets, indicating that the window for transaction-by-transaction tracing and direct recovery of the unfrozen assets has largely closed. — link

text: CME Group's First Weekend of 24/7 Crypto Futures and Options Trading Sees Over 7,200 Traded (~$50 million) CME Group, the world's largest derivatives exchange, announced the launch of 24/7 trading for its cryptocurrency futures and options products. During the first weekend following the launch, more than 7,200 contracts were traded, representing ~$50 million in notional value. CME said the move is intended to meet growing demand for around-the-clock liquidity and risk management tools, while further narrowing the gap between traditional financial markets and the 24/7 trading nature of digital assets. — link
